senses.definition: Situated near the middle line of a body or structure.
examples: In some shells, admedian ridges run close to the central line.
The scientist described an admedian band of color on the insect’s wing.

senses.definition: People who work in advertising, especially men working creatively or in sales for advertisements.
examples: The agency’s admen were brainstorming ideas for the new campaign.
Young admen and adwomen are drawn to the fast-paced world of marketing.

senses.definition: The act or process of measuring something, especially land or dimensions.
examples: Admensuration of the site was required before construction began.
Early surveyors used basic tools for admensuration of fields.

senses.definition: To feel great wonder or astonishment (an obsolete or archaic form of ‘marvel’).
examples: He did admerveylle at the strange sight before him.
They admerveylled at the king’s rich gifts.

senses.definition: A king in Greek mythology, known in stories where his wife Alcestis offers her life for his.
examples: Admetus is often remembered as the husband of Alcestis in Greek myth.
The play tells how Admetus is saved when his wife agrees to die in his place.

senses.definition: Administrative work, or the department that deals with such work in an organization.
examples: I spend too much time on basic admin instead of teaching.
She works in admin at a large insurance company.
The school’s admin needs to process your application first.

senses.definition: A person who manages and controls a system, website, or online group.
examples: Ask the forum admin if you want to change your username.
The server admin will restart the system tonight.

senses.definition: Something that gives help or support, especially in a secondary or minor way.
examples: These notes are only an adminicle to the main report.
The diagram serves as an adminicle to the written explanation.

senses.definition: A piece of evidence that supports a case but is not enough to prove it alone, especially in Scots law.
examples: The letter was treated as an adminicle in the trial.
Several adminicles together established the facts of the case.

senses.definition: Plural of adminicle: things that give help or minor support, or supporting pieces of evidence.
examples: These documents are adminicles that back up the main contract.
The court considered several adminicles before reaching a decision.

senses.definition: Plural of adminiculum: things that support or strengthen something, especially in a minor way.
examples: Various adminicula were added to support the argument.
The second chapter supplies adminicula for the central thesis.

senses.definition: Giving support or help in a secondary or minor way.
examples: The report offers adminicular evidence in favor of the claim.
These adminicular details make the main argument clearer.

senses.definition: In a way that gives minor or supporting help.
examples: The data serve adminiculary to back the main conclusion.
These notes function adminiculary rather than as primary sources.

senses.definition: To support or strengthen something, especially an argument or statement.
examples: Several studies adminiculate the theory presented here.
These statistics adminiculate his position on education reform.

senses.definition: Supported or strengthened, especially by extra evidence or details.
examples: An adminiculated argument is harder to challenge.
His thesis is adminiculated by a wealth of historical examples.

senses.definition: Third person singular of adminiculate: supports or strengthens something, especially by additional evidence.
examples: This new research adminiculates their earlier findings.
The witness’s statement adminiculates the prosecution’s case.

senses.definition: Providing support or extra strength to something, especially to an argument or idea.
examples: Adminiculating the main hypothesis, the author cites several case studies.
The figures adminiculating his view are included in the appendix.

senses.definition: The act of giving support or extra strength to something, especially by adding evidence.
examples: The study offers further adminiculation of the proposed model.
Without adminiculation, his claim remains weak.

senses.definition: A supporting part or structure; something that holds up or helps another thing.
examples: The pillar served as an adminiculum for the arch.
Historical examples act as an adminiculum to the main argument.

senses.definition: To manage and organize the affairs of a company, organization, or system.
examples: The charity is administered by a small board of trustees.
She was hired to administer the company’s new training program.
A central agency administers the national exams.

senses.definition: To give someone medicine, punishment, or a test in an official way.
examples: The nurse will administer the vaccine in the morning.
The teacher administered the test to all the students at once.

senses.definition: Non‑standard or misspelled form of ‘administered’.
examples: The report mistakenly used ‘administerd’ instead of ‘administered’.
In the old note, the word ‘administerd’ appears several times.

senses.definition: Past tense of administer: managed and organized the affairs of something.
examples: The fund was administered by an independent committee.
The estate was carefully administered after his death.

senses.definition: Past tense of administer: gave medicine, a test, or punishment in an official way.
examples: The drug was administered orally twice a day.
The exam was administered under strict conditions.

senses.definition: Relating to or involved in administering or managing, especially government or official affairs.
examples: He took on various administerial responsibilities in the department.
Her administerial duties kept her in the office late.

senses.definition: Managing and organizing the affairs of something.
examples: She is responsible for administering the scholarship program.
The agency is administering several new projects this year.

senses.definition: Giving medicine, a test, or punishment in an official way.
examples: The nurse is administering the vaccine to all patients today.
The teacher is administering the final exam this morning.

senses.definition: Rare plural form referring to acts of administering.
examples: Various administerings of the policy took place over the decade.
The report describes the different administerings of the law in each region.

senses.definition: Third person singular of administer: manages or organizes the affairs of something.
examples: The foundation administers several international scholarships.
A public agency administers the pension scheme.

senses.definition: Third person singular of administer: gives medicine, a test, or punishment in an official way.
examples: The nurse administers the injections every morning.
The school administers national tests once a year.

senses.definition: Able to be administered, especially said of medicine, tasks, or systems.
examples: The drug is administrable by mouth or injection.
We need an administrable plan that staff can follow easily.

senses.definition: A person who administers something, especially a medicine or a religious rite.
examples: The administrant of the sacrament must be properly trained.
The nurse, as administrant of the drug, checked the dosage carefully.

senses.definition: Plural of administrant: people who administer something, such as drugs or religious rites.
examples: The administrants must follow strict safety procedures.
Different administrants performed the rituals in turn.

senses.definition: To manage and organize the affairs of a company, system, or organization.
examples: She was hired to administrate the new training center.
It is difficult to administrate such a large network.

senses.definition: Past tense of administrate: managed or organized the affairs of something.
examples: The project was administrated by an external consultant.
The network was poorly administrated at first.

senses.definition: Third person singular of administrate: manages and organizes the affairs of something.
examples: The IT department administrates all user accounts.
A small team administrates the charity’s activities.

senses.definition: Managing and organizing the affairs of an organization, system, or project.
examples: She is administrating several community programs at once.
Administrating a global network requires careful planning.

senses.definition: The activities of managing and organizing the work of a company, school, or other organization.
examples: Most teachers feel that administration takes too much of their time.
She studied business administration at university.
School administration has introduced a new attendance system.

senses.definition: The government of a country, especially a particular president’s or leader’s period in power.
examples: The new administration has promised tax cuts.
Policies introduced by the previous administration were unpopular.

senses.definition: The act of giving medicine, punishment, or a test in an official way.
examples: Correct administration of the drug is essential.
The administration of justice must be fair and transparent.

senses.definition: Relating to administration or administrative processes.
examples: The report reviews the administrational structure of the university.
They made several administrational changes to improve efficiency.

senses.definition: A person who supports or is closely connected with a particular government administration.
examples: As an administrationist, he defended the president’s policies.
The newspaper accused them of being blind administrationists.

senses.definition: Plural of administration: groups that manage organizations, or periods of government in power.
examples: Different administrations handled the crisis in different ways.
Universities often change their policies under new administrations.

senses.definition: Relating to the work of organizing and managing the operation of a company, institution, or system.
examples: She has an administrative job in the finance department.
The company restructured its administrative staff.
We had to deal with a lot of administrative problems.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to managing and organizing the work of an organization or system.
examples: The project is administratively complex.
Administratively, the school is independent from the university.

senses.definition: A person whose job is to manage and organize the work of a company, school, or other organization.
examples: She works as an administrator in a large hospital.
Good administrators need strong communication skills.
The university appointed a new administrator last year.

senses.definition: A person legally appointed to manage the property or business of someone, especially after bankruptcy or death without a will.
examples: An administrator was appointed to handle the company’s debts.
If there is no will, the court chooses an administrator for the estate.

senses.definition: Plural of administrator: people who manage and organize the work of organizations or systems.
examples: Teachers and administrators met to discuss the new policy.
System administrators are updating the servers tonight.

senses.definition: The position, role, or period of service of an administrator.
examples: Her administratorship lasted for nearly a decade.
The success of his administratorship was widely recognized.

senses.definition: An old term for a female administrator.
examples: The administratress of the estate was his eldest daughter.
In older documents, the word administratress is sometimes used.

senses.definition: Plural of administratrix or administratress: female administrators, especially of estates in legal use.
examples: The wills named two sisters as joint administratrices.
Historically, widows often served as administratrices of family property.

senses.definition: A woman legally appointed to manage the estate of someone who has died without a will.
examples: She was named administratrix of her late husband’s estate.
The court appointed an administratrix to handle the property.

senses.definition: Plural of admin: people who manage systems, websites, or online groups.
examples: The group’s admins removed the spam posts.
Server admins will perform maintenance this weekend.

senses.definition: Plural, informal: departments or teams that handle administrative work.
examples: Talk to the admins if you need a new access card.
The school’s admins are updating student records.

senses.definition: Common misspelling of ‘administration’.
examples: The word ‘adminstration’ in the report should be corrected to ‘administration’.
He typed ‘adminstration’ by mistake in the email.

senses.definition: Plural of admirability: qualities that make someone or something worthy of admiration.
examples: Her many admirabilities include courage and patience.
The book’s admirabilities lie in its honesty and clarity.

senses.definition: The quality of being worthy of admiration or respect.
examples: His admirability lies in his calm leadership.
They spoke about the admirability of her efforts during the crisis.

senses.definition: Deserving respect and approval because of having good or impressive qualities.
examples: She has done an admirable job in difficult circumstances.
His patience with the children was truly admirable.
It is admirable that they admitted their mistake so quickly.

senses.definition: The state or quality of being admirable.
examples: The admirableness of her character inspired everyone around her.
He wrote about the admirableness of their quiet bravery.
